title: Frame‑Rate Comparison (24/25/30/60 fps) & Subset Strategies
content_html:
Frame‑Rate Comparison & Subset Strategies

Timestamp: Sunday, 31 August 2025 at 05:56 PM Prompt: Create a new Excel and HTML that add durations for 24/25/30/60 fps and explain the visual differences.

Download the Excel (with 24/25/30/60 fps durations):

permutation_subsets_fps_24_25_30_60.xlsx

Visual Differences Between Frame Rates

  • 24 fps (film): cinematic cadence with modest motion blur and slight stutter; widely associated with narrative cinema.
  • 25 fps (PAL): similar to 24 fps but fractionally smoother; common in European broadcast.
  • 30 fps (NTSC): noticeably smoother and “live”; good for news, vlogs, and general web video.
  • 60 fps (HFR): very smooth and clear during fast motion; ideal for sports/gaming; can feel “hyper‑real” for drama.

Duration Formula

For a set with F frames at frame rate r fps, minutes = F / (r × 60).

What’s in the Excel

  • Summary sheet lists each subset’s frame count and durations at 24/25/30/60 fps.
  • Strategy sheets include a “Durations” line and a “Notes” column:
    • SJT_first_500 — minimal‑change (adjacent swap) order; smooth transitions.
    • Lexi_stride_10 — every 10th permutation in lexicographic order.
    • Lexi_random_500 — uniform random sample (seed=42) for reproducibility.
    • Lexi_spaced_500 — evenly spaced indices across the full range.
    • Derangements_7 / Derangements_8 — no fixed points.
    • HammingBalanced_500 — greedy farthest‑point sampling to maximize variety.

Tip: For motion‑design, choose 24/25 fps for a cinematic feel, 30 fps for general smoothness, and 60 fps for ultra‑smooth, high‑clarity motion.
